title: thylocodraba thylacocarpa
side_effects: Thylocodraba thylacocarpa can have side effects if used improperly, such as inducing dizziness and nausea due to its high concentration of thylacocarpic acid. Prolonged exposure to the plant's volatile oils may also cause respiratory issues, including shortness of breath and allergic reactions. In some cases, improper use of the plant has been linked to gastrointestinal problems, including diarrhea and stomach cramps. Overconsumption of the plant's leaves can also lead to an imbalance of electrolytes in the body, resulting in muscle weakness and fatigue.
